# UC-18.3.19 · Spine-Leaf ECMP Member Utilization Balance

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Performance, Capacity, Operational &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We compare traffic on each spine-ward uplink so one cable group cannot flood while its partners stay almost idle, which helps teams spot trouble before big copy jobs or storage bursts hurt apps.*

---

## Description

Correlates NX-OS uplink telemetry (per-member transmit and receive rates from nxos:interface_stats, ASIC forwarding-distribution shares from nxos:ecmp_distribution), switch health tiles, NX-OS syslog polarization and port-channel signals, and BGP neighbor posture to expose ECMP imbalance ratios, percentage-point skew versus equal-cost 1/N expectations, and joined hints that separate benign long-lived flows from persistent hot-spine pinning on leaf-to-spine bundles.

## Value

When parallel spine paths stay lopsided for hours, a single uplink absorbs elephant flows and contention shows up as latency or drops before classic routing alarms fire. Quantifying member splits early gives NetOps time to tune hash inputs, shift workloads, or stage capacity before storage replication or backup jobs surprise the business.

## Implementation

Ingest all five sourcetypes on governed indexes with 60–120s NX-API or HEC cadences; FIELDALIAS legacy counter names into tx_bps, rx_bps, and member_if; publish fabric_ecmp_inventory.csv with suppression windows; schedule uc_18_3_19_ecmp_balance_core and clone uc_18_3_19_ecmp_balance_alert with per leaf/spine throttles.

## Detailed Implementation

**Prerequisites**

- Install **Splunk REST API Modular Input** ([Splunkbase **1546**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)) on Heavy Forwarders facing NX-API or eAPI with TLS; store secrets in `passwords.conf`, never inside saved-search definitions.
- Add **Cisco Data Center Networking for Splunk Platform** ([Splunkbase **7777**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/7777)) as a visual reference, then rebind macros to your `dc_fabric` / `network_ops` / `network` indexes and the five sourcetypes in this use case.
- When leaves hang from APIC, enrich inventory using **Splunk Add-on for Cisco ACI** ([Splunkbase **4022**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4022))—that feed contextualizes tenants but does not replace per-member counters from NX-OS.
- Optional calendars: **Splunk Add-on for VMware NSX** ([Splunkbase **4856**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)) and **Splunk Add-on for VMware** ([Splunkbase **1810**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1810)) explain correlated east-west bursts during compute motion; they are overlays, not substitutes for switch telemetry.
- Grant read-only automation accounts that can execute `show port-channel traffic`, `show forwarding distribution`, and `show ip route summary` (or JSON equivalents) without configuration privileges.
- Keep lab traffic out of production indexes, align clocks with NTP, and maintain `fabric_ecmp_inventory.csv` with `suppress_until`, `asic_generation`, and notes from recent hash-seed or resilient-ECMP changes.

**Step 1 — Configure data collection**

- **`nxos:interface_stats`** — Poll NX-API `ins_api` or gNMI OpenConfig interfaces every **60–120s** for each port-channel member facing a spine. Capture **`tx_bps`**, **`rx_bps`**, or monotonic octets plus **`poll_interval_sec`** so Splunk can derive rates. Ground-truth with **`show port-channel traffic`** and **`show interface`** rates during baselines.
- **`nxos:ecmp_distribution`** — Ingest structured output from **`show forwarding distribution`** (and interface-scoped variants) that exposes per-next-hop share or bucket weighting for ECMP groups toward spine loopbacks. Normalize **`member_tx_share_pct`**, **`fwd_tx_skew_pct`**, **`ecmp_width`**, and peer identifiers so ASIC-reported skew is visible even when SNMP summaries look flat.
- **`network:switch_health`** — Fold NMS or agent JSON into a single sourcetype with **`health_status`**, **`hostname`**, and meaningful alarm text for degraded link or supervisor states that often accompany (but do not prove) ECMP polarization.
- **`cisco:nxos:syslog`** — Forward platform lines referencing **ECMP**, resilient hash, port-channel, **L3VM**, **adj-mgr**, **CRC**, or link-down sequences via SC4S or a Universal Forwarder; preserve facility and mnemonic fields for correlation.
- **`network:bgp_neighbor`** — Track EVPN peer finite state, last event, and prefix counters so routing reconvergence bursts can be distinguished from persistent physical imbalance on uplinks.
- Size collectors for roughly two-minute sampling at under 10 MB/day/leaf with compression; burst only when full **`show tech`** exports are intentionally avoided.

**Step 2 — Create the search and alert**

Save **`uc_18_3_19_ecmp_balance_core`**, clone **`uc_18_3_19_ecmp_balance_alert`** for paging, and wrap index unions inside **`macro_dc_fabric_indexes`**.

```spl
(index=dc_fabric OR index=network_ops OR index=network) sourcetype="nxos:interface_stats" earliest=-2h@m latest=now
| eval leaf=trim(lower(coalesce(hostname,host,device_name,switch_id,nms_hostname,"")))
| eval spine_peer=trim(lower(coalesce(spine_peer,ecmp_peer,nh_peer,spine_hostname,"")))
| eval member_if=trim(coalesce(member_if,interface_name,if_name,port_channel_member,"")))
| eval fabric_token=trim(coalesce(fabric_token,pod_id,site_code,"default"))
| eval tx_bps=tonumber(coalesce(tx_bps,out_bps,bits_out_per_sec,transmit_bps,""))
| eval rx_bps=tonumber(coalesce(rx_bps,in_bps,bits_in_per_sec,receive_bps,""))
| eval service_bps=if(isnotnull(tx_bps), tx_bps, rx_bps)
| bin _time span=5m
| stats avg(service_bps) AS avg_svc_bps avg(tx_bps) AS avg_tx avg(rx_bps) AS avg_rx max(tx_bps) AS peak_tx max(rx_bps) AS peak_rx BY _time fabric_token leaf spine_peer member_if
| eventstats sum(avg_svc_bps) AS bundle_total dc(member_if) AS ecmp_width BY _time fabric_token leaf spine_peer
| eval expected_pct=if(ecmp_width>0, 100/ecmp_width, null())
| eval share_pct=if(bundle_total>0, round(100*avg_svc_bps/bundle_total,3), null())
| eval skew_pts=if(isnotnull(expected_pct) AND isnotnull(share_pct), abs(share_pct-expected_pct), null())
| eventstats max(avg_svc_bps) AS hi_svc min(avg_svc_bps) AS lo_svc BY _time fabric_token leaf spine_peer
| eval imbalance_ratio=if(lo_svc>0, round(hi_svc/lo_svc,2), null())
| join type=left fabric_token leaf _time [
    search (index=dc_fabric OR index=network_ops OR index=network) earliest=-2h@m latest=now (
      sourcetype="nxos:ecmp_distribution" OR sourcetype="network:switch_health" OR sourcetype="cisco:nxos:syslog" OR sourcetype="network:bgp_neighbor"
    )
    | bin _time span=5m
    | eval fabric_token=trim(coalesce(fabric_token,pod_id,site_code,"default"))
    | eval leaf=trim(lower(coalesce(hostname,host,device_name,switch_id,nms_hostname,"")))
    | eval vendor_skew=tonumber(coalesce(fwd_tx_skew_pct,ecmp_skew_pct,distrib_imbalance_pct,""))
    | eval polar_sys=if(sourcetype=="cisco:nxos:syslog" AND match(_raw,"(?i)(ECMP|polar|resilient|hash|port-channel|member|L3VM|adj-mgr)"),1,0)
    | eval health_bad=if(sourcetype=="network:switch_health" AND match(lower(coalesce(health_status,status,"")),"critical|major|down|degraded"),1,0)
    | eval bgp_bad=if(sourcetype=="network:bgp_neighbor" AND NOT match(lower(coalesce(bgp_state,neighbor_state,"")),"established"),1,0)
    | stats max(vendor_skew) AS vendor_skew_pct sum(polar_sys) AS syslog_polar_hints max(health_bad) AS health_alarm max(bgp_bad) AS bgp_alarm BY _time fabric_token leaf
]
| eval polar_hint=if(syslog_polar_hints>=2 OR vendor_skew_pct>=15 OR (health_alarm==1 AND skew_pts>=12),1,0)
| eval min_bps_floor=5000000
| where bundle_total>=min_bps_floor AND skew_pts>=18 AND (imbalance_ratio>=2.2 OR polar_hint==1 OR skew_pts>=25)
| sort - skew_pts - imbalance_ratio - _time
| head 300
| table _time fabric_token leaf spine_peer member_if ecmp_width share_pct expected_pct skew_pts imbalance_ratio avg_tx avg_rx peak_tx vendor_skew_pct syslog_polar_hints health_alarm bgp_alarm bundle_total polar_hint
```

**Understanding this SPL** — The outer search scopes **`nxos:interface_stats`** and derives **`service_bps`**, preferring transmit bitrate on northbound uplinks. The **`bin`** command builds **five-minute** windows that tame micro-bursts from elephant flows while remaining responsive across shift changes. **`stats`** averages member rates per leaf, spine peer, fabric token, and time bucket. **`eventstats`** sums **`bundle_total`** and counts distinct **`member_if`** values as **`ecmp_width`**. Expected share is **equal-cost 1/N** in percentage points; **`share_pct`** is the observed split and **`skew_pts`** captures deviation from hashing theory. A second **`eventstats`** pass computes **`hi_svc`** and **`lo_svc`** so **`imbalance_ratio`** (max divided by min) highlights hot-member pinning.

The **`join`** pulls summarized rows from **`nxos:ecmp_distribution`**, **`network:switch_health`**, **`cisco:nxos:syslog`**, and **`network:bgp_neighbor`**, rolling vendor skew **`vendor_skew_pct`**, syslog polarization hits **`syslog_polar_hints`**, **`health_alarm`**, and **`bgp_alarm`** per leaf bucket. **`polar_hint`** upgrades confidence when polarization evidence appears beyond rate skew alone. **`min_bps_floor`** filters idle bundles so low-rate noise does not page on-call.

**Pipeline walkthrough** — Normalize identity fields; aggregate interface rates; compute expected versus observed share, **`skew_pts`**, and **`imbalance_ratio`**; left-join context sourcetypes; filter meaningful skew; sort worst-first; render investigator columns without credentials or private BGP details in the final **`table`**.

**Step 3 — Validate**

- Compare Splunk **`share_pct`** against **`show forwarding distribution`** screenshots and tolerate roughly **three percentage points** of drift when CLI sampling cadence differs from five-minute averages.
- Reconcile **`show port-channel traffic`** member counters with **`avg_tx`** / **`avg_rx`**; mismatches usually indicate FIELDALIAS gaps or standby-supervisor polling, not fabric faults.
- Validate that **`show ip route summary`** **ECMP** counts align with **`ecmp_width`** returned by Splunk; transient churn should coincide with **`network:bgp_neighbor`** events rather than multi-day static skew.
- Run controlled lab traffic (symmetric mice versus long-hash elephants) and document expected **`imbalance_ratio`** ceilings for each ASIC generation you operate.
- If post-upgrade polls flatline, replay NX-API manually before assuming the fabric healed—most failures are RBAC, TLS pinning, or IP reachability, not silent balancing.

**Step 4 — Operationalize**

Wire dashboard tokens for **`fabric_token`**, **`leaf`**, **`spine_peer`**, and **`member_if`**; schedule hourly CSV exports for capacity partners; map results into CMDB-owned **`fabric_ecmp_inventory.csv`** without embedding operator secrets in tokens or drilldown URLs.

**Step 5 — Visualization, alerting, and troubleshooting**

**Visualization** — (1) Heatmap of **`member_if × leaf`** colored by **`skew_pts`**. (2) Scatter of **`share_pct`** versus **`expected_pct`** split by spine peer. (3) Timechart of **`imbalance_ratio`** with **`polar_hint`** markers. (4) Table of worst rows including **vendor skew**, **syslog hints**, **health**, and **BGP** flags. (5) Single value of worst **`imbalance_ratio`** per **`fabric_token`**.

**Alert design** — Evaluate every **10 minutes** over a rolling **two-hour** window. Fire when **`skew_pts ≥ 25`** for two consecutive runs or **`imbalance_ratio ≥ 3.5`** with **`bundle_total`** above the floor. Throttle **45 minutes** per **`leaf`/`spine_peer`** pair, honor **`suppress_until`** during documented port-channel rebuilds, and send payloads with rates, skew, imbalance ratio, and recent syslog mnemonics—never API passwords or private AS numbers.

**Troubleshooting** — Null **`tx_bps`** after upgrades: refresh FIELDALIAS for renamed NX-API keys. Join duplicates: verify **`fabric_token`** cardinality. Skew without syslog hits: inspect flow entropy and application hashing. Healthy BGP with one hot member: look for interface errors on that physical link. High vendor skew but flat interface stats: validate **`nxos:ecmp_distribution`** parser version against ASIC release notes.


## SPL

```spl
(index=dc_fabric OR index=network_ops OR index=network) sourcetype="nxos:interface_stats" earliest=-2h@m latest=now
| eval leaf=trim(lower(coalesce(hostname,host,device_name,switch_id,nms_hostname,"")))
| eval spine_peer=trim(lower(coalesce(spine_peer,ecmp_peer,nh_peer,spine_hostname,"")))
| eval member_if=trim(coalesce(member_if,interface_name,if_name,port_channel_member,"")))
| eval fabric_token=trim(coalesce(fabric_token,pod_id,site_code,"default"))
| eval tx_bps=tonumber(coalesce(tx_bps,out_bps,bits_out_per_sec,transmit_bps,""))
| eval rx_bps=tonumber(coalesce(rx_bps,in_bps,bits_in_per_sec,receive_bps,""))
| eval service_bps=if(isnotnull(tx_bps), tx_bps, rx_bps)
| bin _time span=5m
| stats avg(service_bps) AS avg_svc_bps avg(tx_bps) AS avg_tx avg(rx_bps) AS avg_rx max(tx_bps) AS peak_tx max(rx_bps) AS peak_rx BY _time fabric_token leaf spine_peer member_if
| eventstats sum(avg_svc_bps) AS bundle_total dc(member_if) AS ecmp_width BY _time fabric_token leaf spine_peer
| eval expected_pct=if(ecmp_width>0, 100/ecmp_width, null())
| eval share_pct=if(bundle_total>0, round(100*avg_svc_bps/bundle_total,3), null())
| eval skew_pts=if(isnotnull(expected_pct) AND isnotnull(share_pct), abs(share_pct-expected_pct), null())
| eventstats max(avg_svc_bps) AS hi_svc min(avg_svc_bps) AS lo_svc BY _time fabric_token leaf spine_peer
| eval imbalance_ratio=if(lo_svc>0, round(hi_svc/lo_svc,2), null())
| join type=left fabric_token leaf _time [
    search (index=dc_fabric OR index=network_ops OR index=network) earliest=-2h@m latest=now (
      sourcetype="nxos:ecmp_distribution" OR sourcetype="network:switch_health" OR sourcetype="cisco:nxos:syslog" OR sourcetype="network:bgp_neighbor"
    )
    | bin _time span=5m
    | eval fabric_token=trim(coalesce(fabric_token,pod_id,site_code,"default"))
    | eval leaf=trim(lower(coalesce(hostname,host,device_name,switch_id,nms_hostname,"")))
    | eval vendor_skew=tonumber(coalesce(fwd_tx_skew_pct,ecmp_skew_pct,distrib_imbalance_pct,""))
    | eval polar_sys=if(sourcetype=="cisco:nxos:syslog" AND match(_raw,"(?i)(ECMP|polar|resilient|hash|port-channel|member|L3VM|adj-mgr)"),1,0)
    | eval health_bad=if(sourcetype=="network:switch_health" AND match(lower(coalesce(health_status,status,"")),"critical|major|down|degraded"),1,0)
    | eval bgp_bad=if(sourcetype=="network:bgp_neighbor" AND NOT match(lower(coalesce(bgp_state,neighbor_state,"")),"established"),1,0)
    | stats max(vendor_skew) AS vendor_skew_pct sum(polar_sys) AS syslog_polar_hints max(health_bad) AS health_alarm max(bgp_bad) AS bgp_alarm BY _time fabric_token leaf
]
| eval polar_hint=if(syslog_polar_hints>=2 OR vendor_skew_pct>=15 OR (health_alarm==1 AND skew_pts>=12),1,0)
| eval min_bps_floor=5000000
| where bundle_total>=min_bps_floor AND skew_pts>=18 AND (imbalance_ratio>=2.2 OR polar_hint==1 OR skew_pts>=25)
| sort - skew_pts - imbalance_ratio - _time
| head 300
| table _time fabric_token leaf spine_peer member_if ecmp_width share_pct expected_pct skew_pts imbalance_ratio avg_tx avg_rx peak_tx vendor_skew_pct syslog_polar_hints health_alarm bgp_alarm bundle_total polar_hint
```

## Visualization

(1) Heatmap — member_if × leaf colored by skew_pts. (2) Scatter — share_pct vs expected_pct by spine_peer. (3) Timechart — imbalance_ratio with polar_hint markers. (4) Table — worst rows with vendor skew, syslog hints, health, BGP flags. (5) Single value — worst imbalance_ratio per fabric_token.

## Known False Positives

**_rolling_code_upgrade_windows** — During ISSU or supervisor reloads, forwarding-distribution snapshots pause while linecards reseat buckets, inflating skew for minutes without real overload. Require polar_hint=0 and two clean polls after the change record closes, or honor suppress_until in fabric_ecmp_inventory.

**_symmetric_elephant_flow_pinning** — Long-lived TCP sessions (backup agents, replication) can ride one member legitimately because per-flow hashing never re-seeds. Imbalance persists yet drop counters stay quiet. Compare imbalance_ratio history with application owners before escalating hardware.

**_telemetry_alias_drift** — TA upgrades sometimes rename out_bps or nest member_if under interface; tonumber() yields null while CLI still looks hot. Run fieldsummary after each upgrade and retain FIELDALIAS for legacy sourcetypes.

**_vpc_mclag_asymmetry** — One vPC or MC-LAG peer can reflect more east-west traffic than its partner, mimicking bad ECMP toward spines. Cross-check vpc consistency on both leaf names before blaming spine hashing.

**_stale_distribution_batches** — Hourly show forwarding distribution dumps can mis-align _time buckets with real-time interface_stats, creating phantom vendor skew. Stamp ingest_lag_sec and drop join rows older than two windows.

**_bgp_refresh_waves** — Soft inbound refreshes briefly reshuffle next-hops while neighbors remain established. Correlate automation job IDs and damp alerts unless skew_pts stays high past documented RIB stabilization times.

**_nms_false_critical** — network:switch_health may mark critical when SNMP is blocked, not when uplinks saturate. Require nxos:interface_stats confirmation before hardware dispatch.

**_standby_supervisor_polls** — Dual-SUP chassis sometimes return idle counters from standby contexts when hostname lacks active_role. Filter standby polls using redundancy_state before balance math.
